configuration.description:
Complete structured configuration for a Fred agent pod.

Mirrors agentic-backend's `Configuration` model, scoped to what an agent
pod actually needs. All field types are reused from fred-core and fred-sdk
— no new types invented here.

How to use it:
- write a `config/configuration.yaml` alongside `config/models_catalog.yaml`
  and `config/mcp_catalog.yaml`
- call `load_agent_pod_config()` at startup
- pass the result to `create_agent_app(config=...)`

The `security` field is a `SecurityConfiguration` from fred-core, the same
type used in agentic-backend. When `security.user.enabled` is True,
`create_agent_app` enforces Keycloak JWT validation on every endpoint.

app.description:
Basic HTTP server and local observability settings for an agent pod.

Why this exists:
- every pod needs the same HTTP binding knobs plus the small Prometheus/KPI
  settings already used by the other Fred backends
- keeping these fields in `app` preserves the familiar startup contract for
  local benches and scrape-based debugging

How to use it:
- keep the defaults for simple local development
- set `metrics_port` / `metrics_address` when `observability.metrics` is
  `prometheus`

Example:
- `PodAppConfig(base_url="/pod/v1", port=8000, metrics_port=9115)`

limit_concurrency.description: Optional maximum number of concurrent HTTP or WebSocket connections accepted by Uvicorn. Leave unset to disable the limit.
limit_concurrency.title: Limit Concurrency

app.title: PodAppConfig

oneOf.description: Configuration for an OpenFGA-backed relationship engine.

enabled.description: To disable ReBAC checks (do not disable in production). If OIDC (UserSecurity and M2MSecurity) ReBAC check will be disabled even if this is true.
enabled.title: Enabled

api_url.description: Base URL for the OpenFGA HTTP API (e.g. https://fga.example.com)
api_url.title: Api Url

store_name.description: Name of the OpenFGA store to use
store_name.title: Store Name

authorization_model_id.description: Optional authorization model ID to use for read operations. Will be overridden if sync_schema_on_init is True.
authorization_model_id.title: Authorization Model Id

create_store_if_needed.description: Create the OpenFGA store if it does not already exist
create_store_if_needed.title: Create Store If Needed

sync_schema_on_init.description: Synchronize the authorization model when creating the engine
sync_schema_on_init.title: Sync Schema On Init

token_env_var.description: Environment variable that stores the OpenFGA API token
token_env_var.title: Token Env Var

timeout_millisec.description: Timeout in milliseconds for OpenFGA API requests. Defaults to 5000 so a stalled OpenFGA call fails fast with an error instead of hanging the request indefinitely (set to None only to explicitly disable the timeout).
timeout_millisec.title: Timeout Millisec

headers.description: Static HTTP headers to send with each OpenFGA API request
headers.title: Headers

oneOf.title: OpenFgaRebacConfig

ai.description:
AI model and knowledge-flow settings for an agent pod.

Why this section exists separately from AgentAppSettings:
- pods only need the outbound Knowledge Flow base URL plus runtime HTTP
  timeout tuning shared by KF and MCP adapters
- gateway-only concerns such as attachment/session limits should not leak
  into the pod config contract

Example:
- `PodAIConfig(knowledge_flow_url="http://localhost:8111/knowledge-flow/v1", timeout=RuntimeTimeouts(connect=20, read=60))`

timeout.description:
Minimal timeout settings for outbound runtime HTTP clients.

Why this exists:
- KF/MCP clients need consistent timeout settings across runtimes

How to use it:
- construct with explicit connect/read values
- call `as_httpx_timeout_config()` to feed httpx
timeout.title: RuntimeTimeouts

ai.title: PodAIConfig

observability.description:
Observability provider selection for a Fred agent pod.

Non-secret settings (backend choice, endpoints) live in configuration.yaml.
Credentials (API keys, tokens) stay in the .env file.

Example:
    observability:
      tracer: logging       # null | logging | langfuse
      kpi:
        log:
          enabled: true
        prometheus:
          enabled: false
        opensearch:
          enabled: false
      langfuse:
        host: "http://localhost:3001"
        # LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY and LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY in .env

tracer.description:
Distributed tracing backend for the agent pod.

- null     — no tracing, all spans are dropped
- logging  — each span is emitted as a structured log entry (default)
- langfuse — spans are sent to a Langfuse server;
             credentials (LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY) must be
             set in the .env file; host is configured in the langfuse section
tracer.title: TracerBackend

kpi.description:
KPI sink configuration shared across all Fred backends.

Defaults enable Prometheus + OpenSearch (prod-ready out of the box).
For local dev, disable both and enable log instead:

    observability:
      kpi:
        log:
          enabled: true
        prometheus:
          enabled: false
        opensearch:
          enabled: false

process_metrics_interval_sec.description: Emit process/SQL-pool KPIs every N seconds. 0 to disable.
process_metrics_interval_sec.title: Process Metrics Interval Sec

kpi.title: KpiObservabilityConfig

langfuse.description:
Langfuse connection settings.

Only non-secret settings live here.
Credentials go in the .env file as LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY and LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY.
langfuse.title: LangfuseObservabilityConfig

observability.title: PodObservabilityConfig

storage.description:
Persistence backend settings for an agent pod.

All conversation state (sessions, multi-turn history, checkpoints) is
managed exclusively by the LangGraph SQL checkpointer, which uses the
`postgres` connection. There are no separate session or history tables.

Fields:
- `postgres`: SQL engine used by the LangGraph checkpointer (SQLite in
  local dev via sqlite_path, PostgreSQL in production via host/port/database)
- `opensearch`: optional, for log forwarding in production
- `log_store`: optional, for structured log persistence

sqlite_path.description: Path to the SQLite database file (for local dev/testing).
sqlite_path.title: Sqlite Path

pool_size.description: Optional pool size for the engine.
pool_size.title: Pool Size

max_overflow.description: Optional max_overflow for SQLAlchemy pool (defaults to SQLAlchemy's 10 if unset).
max_overflow.title: Max Overflow

pool_timeout.description: Seconds to wait for a connection from the pool before timing out.
pool_timeout.title: Pool Timeout

pool_recycle.description: Recycle connections after this many seconds (prevents stale TCP / server timeouts).
pool_recycle.title: Pool Recycle

pool_pre_ping.description: Enable SQLAlchemy pool_pre_ping to evict stale connections.
pool_pre_ping.title: Pool Pre Ping

storage.title: PodStorageConfig

scheduler.description:
Temporal scheduler settings for an agent pod.

Disabled by default — only needed for Story B (deep/durable agents).
Reuses TemporalSchedulerConfig from fred-core.

backend.description:
Allowed scheduler backend values across Fred backends.

Why this exists:
- Avoid raw string literals (`"temporal"`, `"memory"`) spread in business code.
- Keep one typed source of truth used by configuration parsing and runtime checks.

How to use:
```python
from fred_core.scheduler import SchedulerBackend

if backend == SchedulerBackend.MEMORY:
    ...
```
backend.title: SchedulerBackend

ingestion_workflow_parallelism.description: Max number of files launched in parallel per parent ingestion workflow.
ingestion_workflow_parallelism.title: Ingestion Workflow Parallelism

ingestion_max_concurrent_workflow_tasks.description: Max concurrent Temporal workflow tasks processed by a worker process.
ingestion_max_concurrent_workflow_tasks.title: Ingestion Max Concurrent Workflow Tasks

ingestion_max_concurrent_activities.description: Max concurrent Temporal activities processed by a worker process.
ingestion_max_concurrent_activities.title: Ingestion Max Concurrent Activities

scheduler.title: PodSchedulerConfig

platform.description:
Small set of Fred platform service URLs needed by the pod runtime.

Why this exists:
- agent pods stay execution-focused, but some execution paths still need a
  central Fred service such as control-plane for managed agent-instance
  resolution

How to use it:
- set `control_plane_url` when the pod should accept `agent_instance_id`
  execution requests

Example:
- `PodPlatformConfig(control_plane_url="http://localhost:8222/control-plane/v1")`
platform.title: PodPlatformConfig

configuration.title: AgentPodConfig

personal_max_resources_storage_size.title: Personal Max Resources Storage Size

items.description:
Configuration structure for a file processor.
Attributes:
    suffix (str): The file extension this processor handles (e.g., '.pdf').
    class_path (str): Dotted import path of the processor class.
    description (str): Human readable explanation of what the processor does.

suffix.description: The file extension this processor handles (e.g., '.pdf')
suffix.title: Suffix

class_path.description: Dotted import path of the processor class
class_path.title: Class Path

description.description: Human-readable description of the processor purpose shown in the UI.
description.title: Description

items.title: ProcessorConfig

attachment_processors.description: Optional fast-text processors for attachments. Uses the same ProcessorConfig structure, but classes must subclass BaseFastTextProcessor. If omitted, the default fast processor is used.
attachment_processors.title: Attachment Processors

source_tags.description: Optional source tags this guardrail applies to. Empty means all sources.
source_tags.title: Source Tags

allowed_labels.description: Optional allow-list of labels accepted by the guardrail. Empty means detection-only.
allowed_labels.title: Allowed Labels

on_no_label.description: Behavior when no explicit document marking is detected.
on_no_label.title: On No Label

patterns.description: Regex patterns used to recognize explicit document markings.

label.description: Normalized label returned when the regex matches.
label.title: Label

pattern.description: Regex used to detect the marking in extracted guardrail text.
pattern.title: Pattern

patterns.title: Patterns

document_guardrail.description: Optional ingestion-time guardrail for explicit document markings.

public_endpoint.description: Public MinIO endpoint for browser-facing presigned URLs (e.g. 'https://my.minio.ingress'). If not set, uses endpoint.
public_endpoint.title: Public Endpoint

public_secure.description: Use TLS for public endpoint. If not set, inferred from public_endpoint scheme.
public_secure.title: Public Secure

oneOf.description:
Google Cloud Storage content store. Authentication uses Application Default
Credentials / Workload Identity (no JSON key required).

Bucket-splitting mirrors MinIO: the configured ``bucket_name`` is suffixed
with ``-documents`` and ``-objects`` (content store) and ``-files`` (namespace
file store). All buckets must already exist.

project_id.description: GCP project id; inferred from ADC when empty.
project_id.title: Project Id

signing_service_account_email.description: Service account email used to sign V4 signed URLs for backend-internal tabular Parquet reads, via IAM signBlob under Workload Identity (no JSON key). Required for content_storage.type=gcs; startup fails clearly when omitted. The Workload Identity service account must hold iam.serviceAccounts.signBlob on this account, which must have storage.objects.get on the objects bucket.
signing_service_account_email.title: Signing Service Account Email

oneOf.title: GcsStorageConfig

tabular_store.description:
Dataset-centric tabular storage settings for the supported tabular runtime.

Why this exists:
- CSV ingestion persists one Parquet artifact per document in the shared
  content store.
- One dedicated config block keeps object keys and query limits explicit.

How to use:
- Configure the block directly under `storage.tabular_store`.
- `artifacts_prefix` namespaces Parquet datasets under the shared
  content-store object area.
- `format` is intentionally fixed to `parquet`.

artifacts_prefix.description: Prefix under content_storage objects where Parquet datasets are stored.
artifacts_prefix.title: Artifacts Prefix

format.description: Physical storage format used for tabular artifacts.
format.title: Format

compression.description: Parquet compression codec used when persisting tabular artifacts.
compression.title: Compression

query.description:
Runtime settings for dataset-centric SQL queries.

Why this exists:
- Tabular querying now runs on transient Parquet datasets rather than
  long-lived SQL tables.
- These values keep query execution bounded and configurable from YAML.

How to use:
- Keep the default `duckdb` engine.
- Tune result limits and backend-internal presigned URL TTL per deployment.

engine.description: Embedded query engine used to read Parquet datasets.
engine.title: Engine

access_mode.description: Primary object-access method for remote tabular artifacts.
access_mode.title: Access Mode

internal_presigned_ttl_seconds.description: TTL in seconds for backend-internal object-storage URLs used by tabular DuckDB reads.
internal_presigned_ttl_seconds.title: Internal Presigned Ttl Seconds

default_max_rows.description: Default preview row limit applied when callers omit max_rows.
default_max_rows.title: Default Max Rows

max_rows.description: Hard cap applied to query result previews.
max_rows.title: Max Rows

query.title: TabularQueryConfig

tabular_store.title: TabularStoreConfig

bulk_size.description: Number of documents to send in each bulk insert request
bulk_size.title: Bulk Size

oneOf.description:
Local, embedded Chroma. No server needed.
- persist_path: folder where Chroma (DuckDB/Parquet) stores data
- collection_name: logical collection for your chunks
- distance: ANN space; 'cosine' matches our UI-friendly similarity
oneOf.title: ChromaVectorStorageConfig

oneOf.description:
PostgreSQL + pgvector backend.
- Uses shared `storage.postgres` connection settings.
- Stores vectors in the default pgvector table under a collection name.
oneOf.title: PgVectorStorageConfig

oneOf.description:
ClickHouse backend.
- Uses shared `storage.clickhouse` connection settings.
- Stores vectors in the configured table.

bulk_size.description: Number of rows per insert batch
bulk_size.title: Bulk Size

oneOf.title: ClickHouseVectorStorageConfig

mcp.description:
Feature toggles for MCP-only HTTP/MCP surfaces.

These do NOT affect core storage backends (e.g., using OpenSearch
as vector store or metadata store). They only control whether
optional monitoring/exploration controllers and their MCP servers
are exposed.

reports_enabled.description: Expose the Reports MCP server (Markdown-first report generation).
reports_enabled.title: Reports Enabled

kpi_enabled.description: Expose the KPI MCP server for querying application KPIs.
kpi_enabled.title: Kpi Enabled

tabular_enabled.description: Expose the Tabular MCP server for SQL/table exploration.
tabular_enabled.title: Tabular Enabled

statistic_enabled.description: Expose the Statistical MCP server for data analysis helpers.
statistic_enabled.title: Statistic Enabled

text_enabled.description: Expose the Text MCP server for semantic vector search.
text_enabled.title: Text Enabled

templates_enabled.description: Expose the Template MCP server for prompts/templates.
templates_enabled.title: Templates Enabled

resources_enabled.description: Expose the Resources MCP server for resource/tag management.
resources_enabled.title: Resources Enabled

opensearch_ops_enabled.description: Expose OpenSearch operational endpoints and the corresponding MCP server.
opensearch_ops_enabled.title: Opensearch Ops Enabled

prometheus_ops_enabled.description: Expose Prometheus operational endpoints and the corresponding MCP server.
prometheus_ops_enabled.title: Prometheus Ops Enabled

neo4j_enabled.description: Expose Neo4j graph exploration endpoints and the corresponding MCP server.
neo4j_enabled.title: Neo4J Enabled

filesystem_enabled.description: Expose agent filesystem utils endpoints and the corresponding MCP server.
filesystem_enabled.title: Filesystem Enabled

filesystem_read_default_limit.description: Default line count returned by filesystem read_file when callers omit limit.
filesystem_read_default_limit.title: Filesystem Read Default Limit

filesystem_read_max_limit.description: Absolute maximum line count accepted by filesystem read_file.
filesystem_read_max_limit.title: Filesystem Read Max Limit

filesystem_read_default_max_chars.description: Default maximum character count returned by filesystem read_file when callers omit max_chars.
filesystem_read_default_max_chars.title: Filesystem Read Default Max Chars

filesystem_read_absolute_max_chars.description: Absolute maximum character count accepted by filesystem read_file.
filesystem_read_absolute_max_chars.title: Filesystem Read Absolute Max Chars

mcp.title: MCPConfig

secure.description: Use TLS for the MinIO client.
secure.title: Secure

Default Credentials / Workload Identity (no JSON key required). The bucket
must already exist; an optional ``prefix`` lets several logical roots share it.

personal_max_resources_storage_size.title: Personal Max Resources Storage Size

kpi.description:
KPI sink configuration shared across all Fred backends.

Defaults enable Prometheus + OpenSearch (prod-ready out of the box).
For local dev, disable both and enable log instead:

    observability:
      kpi:
        log:
          enabled: true
        prometheus:
          enabled: false
        opensearch:
          enabled: false

process_metrics_interval_sec.description: Emit process/SQL-pool KPIs every N seconds. 0 to disable.
process_metrics_interval_sec.title: Process Metrics Interval Sec

kpi.title: KpiObservabilityConfig

oneOf.description:
Google Cloud Storage content store. Authentication uses Application Default
Credentials / Workload Identity (no JSON key required).

Bucket-splitting mirrors MinIO: the configured ``bucket_name`` is suffixed
with ``-documents`` and ``-objects`` (content store) and ``-files`` (namespace
file store). All buckets must already exist.

project_id.description: GCP project id; inferred from ADC when empty.
project_id.title: Project Id

signing_service_account_email.description: Service account email used to sign V4 signed URLs for backend-internal tabular Parquet reads, via IAM signBlob under Workload Identity (no JSON key). Required for content_storage.type=gcs; startup fails clearly when omitted. The Workload Identity service account must hold iam.serviceAccounts.signBlob on this account, which must have storage.objects.get on the objects bucket.
signing_service_account_email.title: Signing Service Account Email

oneOf.title: GcsStorageConfig

backend.description:
Allowed scheduler backend values across Fred backends.

Why this exists:
- Avoid raw string literals (`"temporal"`, `"memory"`) spread in business code.
- Keep one typed source of truth used by configuration parsing and runtime checks.

How to use:
```python
from fred_core.scheduler import SchedulerBackend

if backend == SchedulerBackend.MEMORY:
    ...
```
backend.title: SchedulerBackend

tabular_store.description:
Dataset-centric tabular storage settings for the supported tabular runtime.

Why this exists:
- CSV ingestion persists one Parquet artifact per document in the shared
  content store.
- One dedicated config block keeps object keys and query limits explicit.

How to use:
- Configure the block directly under `storage.tabular_store`.
- `artifacts_prefix` namespaces Parquet datasets under the shared
  content-store object area.
- `format` is intentionally fixed to `parquet`.

artifacts_prefix.description: Prefix under content_storage objects where Parquet datasets are stored.
artifacts_prefix.title: Artifacts Prefix

format.description: Physical storage format used for tabular artifacts.
format.title: Format

compression.description: Parquet compression codec used when persisting tabular artifacts.
compression.title: Compression

query.description:
Runtime settings for dataset-centric SQL queries.

Why this exists:
- Tabular querying now runs on transient Parquet datasets rather than
  long-lived SQL tables.
- These values keep query execution bounded and configurable from YAML.

How to use:
- Keep the default `duckdb` engine.
- Tune result limits and backend-internal presigned URL TTL per deployment.

engine.description: Embedded query engine used to read Parquet datasets.
engine.title: Engine

access_mode.description: Primary object-access method for remote tabular artifacts.
access_mode.title: Access Mode

internal_presigned_ttl_seconds.description: TTL in seconds for backend-internal object-storage URLs used by tabular DuckDB reads.
internal_presigned_ttl_seconds.title: Internal Presigned Ttl Seconds

default_max_rows.description: Default preview row limit applied when callers omit max_rows.
default_max_rows.title: Default Max Rows

max_rows.description: Hard cap applied to query result previews.
max_rows.title: Max Rows

query.title: TabularQueryConfig

tabular_store.title: TabularStoreConfig

bulk_size.description: Number of documents to send in each bulk insert request
bulk_size.title: Bulk Size

oneOf.description:
Local, embedded Chroma. No server needed.
- persist_path: folder where Chroma (DuckDB/Parquet) stores data
- collection_name: logical collection for your chunks
- distance: ANN space; 'cosine' matches our UI-friendly similarity
oneOf.title: ChromaVectorStorageConfig

oneOf.description:
PostgreSQL + pgvector backend.
- Uses shared `storage.postgres` connection settings.
- Stores vectors in the default pgvector table under a collection name.
oneOf.title: PgVectorStorageConfig

oneOf.description:
ClickHouse backend.
- Uses shared `storage.clickhouse` connection settings.
- Stores vectors in the configured table.

bulk_size.description: Number of rows per insert batch
bulk_size.title: Bulk Size

oneOf.title: ClickHouseVectorStorageConfig

mcp.description:
Feature toggles for MCP-only HTTP/MCP surfaces.

These do NOT affect core storage backends (e.g., using OpenSearch
as vector store or metadata store). They only control whether
optional monitoring/exploration controllers and their MCP servers
are exposed.

reports_enabled.description: Expose the Reports MCP server (Markdown-first report generation).
reports_enabled.title: Reports Enabled

kpi_enabled.description: Expose the KPI MCP server for querying application KPIs.
kpi_enabled.title: Kpi Enabled

tabular_enabled.description: Expose the Tabular MCP server for SQL/table exploration.
tabular_enabled.title: Tabular Enabled

statistic_enabled.description: Expose the Statistical MCP server for data analysis helpers.
statistic_enabled.title: Statistic Enabled

text_enabled.description: Expose the Text MCP server for semantic vector search.
text_enabled.title: Text Enabled

templates_enabled.description: Expose the Template MCP server for prompts/templates.
templates_enabled.title: Templates Enabled

resources_enabled.description: Expose the Resources MCP server for resource/tag management.
resources_enabled.title: Resources Enabled

opensearch_ops_enabled.description: Expose OpenSearch operational endpoints and the corresponding MCP server.
opensearch_ops_enabled.title: Opensearch Ops Enabled

prometheus_ops_enabled.description: Expose Prometheus operational endpoints and the corresponding MCP server.
prometheus_ops_enabled.title: Prometheus Ops Enabled

neo4j_enabled.description: Expose Neo4j graph exploration endpoints and the corresponding MCP server.
neo4j_enabled.title: Neo4J Enabled

filesystem_enabled.description: Expose agent filesystem utils endpoints and the corresponding MCP server.
filesystem_enabled.title: Filesystem Enabled

filesystem_read_default_limit.description: Default line count returned by filesystem read_file when callers omit limit.
filesystem_read_default_limit.title: Filesystem Read Default Limit

filesystem_read_max_limit.description: Absolute maximum line count accepted by filesystem read_file.
filesystem_read_max_limit.title: Filesystem Read Max Limit

filesystem_read_default_max_chars.description: Default maximum character count returned by filesystem read_file when callers omit max_chars.
filesystem_read_default_max_chars.title: Filesystem Read Default Max Chars

filesystem_read_absolute_max_chars.description: Absolute maximum character count accepted by filesystem read_file.
filesystem_read_absolute_max_chars.title: Filesystem Read Absolute Max Chars

mcp.title: MCPConfig

secure.description: Use TLS for the MinIO client.
secure.title: Secure

oneOf.description:
Google Cloud Storage virtual filesystem. Authentication uses Application
Default Credentials / Workload Identity (no JSON key required). The bucket
must already exist; an optional ``prefix`` lets several logical roots share it.

prefix.description: Optional key prefix within the bucket (default '').
prefix.title: Prefix

project_id.description: GCP project id; inferred from ADC when empty.
project_id.title: Project Id

oneOf.title: GcsFilesystemConfig

default_team_max_resources_storage_size.description: Default maximum resources storage size in bytes for a team
default_team_max_resources_storage_size.title: Default Team Max Resources Storage Size

personal_max_resources_storage_size.description: Maximum resources storage size in bytes for a personal space
personal_max_resources_storage_size.title: Personal Max Resources Storage Size

platform.description:
Control-plane deployment configuration for product/runtime coordination.

Contains ONLY infrastructure references (which runtime pods exist).
Managed agent instance enrollment (which team has which agents) is
DB-backed and never stored in this deployment config.

frontend.description: Static frontend bootstrap configuration served by control-plane.

feature_flags.description: Typed feature flags exposed to the frontend bootstrap.
feature_flags.title: FrontendFeatureFlags

ui_settings.description: Small typed UI settings surface owned by control-plane.
ui_settings.title: FrontendUiSettings

frontend.title: FrontendBootstrapConfig

knowledge_flow_base_url.description: Server-side base URL used by control-plane when it must orchestrate Knowledge Flow attachment cleanup on behalf of the authenticated user.
knowledge_flow_base_url.title: Knowledge Flow Base Url

items.description: Configured runtime endpoint used for read-only template aggregation.

ingress_prefix.description: Ingress-relative URL prefix for browser-facing runtime access, e.g. /runtime/agents-v2. Required for execution preparation. MUST NOT be a cluster-internal hostname or pod IP.
ingress_prefix.title: Ingress Prefix

items.title: RuntimeCatalogSourceConfig

platform.title: PlatformConfig

backend.description:
Allowed scheduler backend values across Fred backends.

Why this exists:
- Avoid raw string literals (`"temporal"`, `"memory"`) spread in business code.
- Keep one typed source of truth used by configuration parsing and runtime checks.

How to use:
```python
from fred_core.scheduler import SchedulerBackend

if backend == SchedulerBackend.MEMORY:
    ...
```
backend.title: SchedulerBackend

ingestion_workflow_parallelism.description: Max number of files launched in parallel per parent ingestion workflow.
ingestion_workflow_parallelism.title: Ingestion Workflow Parallelism

ingestion_max_concurrent_workflow_tasks.description: Max concurrent Temporal workflow tasks processed by a worker process.
ingestion_max_concurrent_workflow_tasks.title: Ingestion Max Concurrent Workflow Tasks

ingestion_max_concurrent_activities.description: Max concurrent Temporal activities processed by a worker process.
ingestion_max_concurrent_activities.title: Ingestion Max Concurrent Activities

oneOf.description: Configuration for an OpenFGA-backed relationship engine.

enabled.description: To disable ReBAC checks (do not disable in production). If OIDC (UserSecurity and M2MSecurity) ReBAC check will be disabled even if this is true.
enabled.title: Enabled

api_url.description: Base URL for the OpenFGA HTTP API (e.g. https://fga.example.com)
api_url.title: Api Url

store_name.description: Name of the OpenFGA store to use
store_name.title: Store Name

authorization_model_id.description: Optional authorization model ID to use for read operations. Will be overridden if sync_schema_on_init is True.
authorization_model_id.title: Authorization Model Id

create_store_if_needed.description: Create the OpenFGA store if it does not already exist
create_store_if_needed.title: Create Store If Needed

sync_schema_on_init.description: Synchronize the authorization model when creating the engine
sync_schema_on_init.title: Sync Schema On Init

token_env_var.description: Environment variable that stores the OpenFGA API token
token_env_var.title: Token Env Var

timeout_millisec.description: Timeout in milliseconds for OpenFGA API requests. Defaults to 5000 so a stalled OpenFGA call fails fast with an error instead of hanging the request indefinitely (set to None only to explicitly disable the timeout).
timeout_millisec.title: Timeout Millisec

headers.description: Static HTTP headers to send with each OpenFGA API request
headers.title: Headers

oneOf.title: OpenFgaRebacConfig

sqlite_path.description: Path to the SQLite database file (for local dev/testing).
sqlite_path.title: Sqlite Path

pool_size.description: Optional pool size for the engine.
pool_size.title: Pool Size

max_overflow.description: Optional max_overflow for SQLAlchemy pool (defaults to SQLAlchemy's 10 if unset).
max_overflow.title: Max Overflow

pool_timeout.description: Seconds to wait for a connection from the pool before timing out.
pool_timeout.title: Pool Timeout

pool_recycle.description: Recycle connections after this many seconds (prevents stale TCP / server timeouts).
pool_recycle.title: Pool Recycle

pool_pre_ping.description: Enable SQLAlchemy pool_pre_ping to evict stale connections.
pool_pre_ping.title: Pool Pre Ping

secret_key.description: MinIO secret key (from MINIO_SECRET_KEY env by default)
secret_key.title: Secret Key

bucket_name.description: Content store bucket name (suffix '-objects' is used for banner objects)
bucket_name.title: Bucket Name

public_endpoint.description: Optional public endpoint used to generate browser-facing presigned URLs
public_endpoint.title: Public Endpoint

public_secure.description: Optional TLS override for public endpoint (auto-inferred when omitted)
public_secure.title: Public Secure

kpi.description:
KPI sink configuration shared across all Fred backends.

Defaults enable Prometheus + OpenSearch (prod-ready out of the box).
For local dev, disable both and enable log instead:

    observability:
      kpi:
        log:
          enabled: true
        prometheus:
          enabled: false
        opensearch:
          enabled: false

process_metrics_interval_sec.description: Emit process/SQL-pool KPIs every N seconds. 0 to disable.
process_metrics_interval_sec.title: Process Metrics Interval Sec

kpi.title: KpiObservabilityConfig

default_team_max_resources_storage_size.description: Default maximum resources storage size in bytes for a team
default_team_max_resources_storage_size.title: Default Team Max Resources Storage Size

personal_max_resources_storage_size.description: Maximum resources storage size in bytes for a personal space
personal_max_resources_storage_size.title: Personal Max Resources Storage Size
